content: I showed up ten minutes early, dressed decently, ready for the usual awkward small talk. The interviewer—let’s call him Bob—started by asking if I could juggle. Not metaphorically, literally. He pulled out three tennis balls from his desk. I laughed, thinking it was a joke. He wasn’t. After an embarrassing attempt, I admitted I couldn’t, and he frowned like I’d failed a critical test. Then came the “stress test,” which was just Bob yelling random numbers while I took notes. When I asked if I could clarify the role’s expectations, he waved me off and said, “You’ll figure it out if you get the job.” The interview ended with Bob asking if I wanted a snack—then offering stale chips from a half-empty bag. It was chaotic, pointless, and honestly, I walked out more confused than enlightened. Not the worst job interview ever, but definitely the most bizarre—and a solid reminder some things don’t deserve overthinking.
